settings.merge_by_version_check: Group and merge packs by Minecraft version (recommended to avoid script conflicts)
settings.merge_by_version_tooltip: When enabled, Start Process will group selected packs by their min_engine_version (script/Minecraft version). Each version group is merged separately: same-version packs → one behavior_pack + resource_pack per version (e.g. 1.20.0/, 1.21.0/). Reduces conflicts between different script API versions.
settings.background_music_tooltip: 从 music/ 文件夹播放无版权 lofi 音乐（如 background.ogg）。需要 pygame。

compatibility.title: 兼容性检查
compatibility.all_have_manifest: 所选 MCPack 均包含清单。
addons_used.title: 本次合并使用的附加包
help.usage1_desc:
Before merging, test each addon separately in Minecraft to ensure they work correctly.
This helps identify problematic addons before merging and saves troubleshooting time.
help.usage2_desc:
Click the '➕ Add Files' button to open a file browser.
Select one or more .mcpack files (hold Ctrl/Cmd to select multiple).
Only .mcpack files are supported. The selected files will appear in the list showing only filenames.
help.usage3_desc:
Click '🔍 Check Packs' to see which Minecraft version each addon requires.
The tool will categorize addons by version (1.16, 1.17, 1.18, 1.19, 1.20, 1.21+).
IMPORTANT: Merging addons from different versions is NOT RECOMMENDED. Each addon is designed to work with a specific version of Minecraft, and merging them from different versions can lead to conflicts, outdated code, missing dependencies, and compatibility issues. Always merge addons from the same version to ensure compatibility and avoid problems.
help.usage4_desc:
After checking versions, organize your addons:
• Create separate folders for each version (e.g., '1.20 Packs', '1.21 Packs')
• ONLY select addons from ONE version at a time for merging
• Never mix versions - merge addons from the same version together
• Create separate merged packs for each version if you have addons from multiple versions
help.usage5_desc:
Click 'Browse' next to the output directory field.
Choose where you want the merged pack files to be saved.
The tool will create 'resource_pack.zip' and 'behavior_pack.zip' in this location.
help.usage6_desc:
If an addon contains multiple subpacks, a selection window will appear.
Choose which subpack you want to include in the merge.
You can only select one subpack per addon file.
help.usage7_desc:
Click '🚀 Start Process' to begin merging.
The progress bar will show 4 steps:
  • Step 1/4: Creating manifest - Generates the merged pack manifest
  • Step 2/4: Processing files - Extracts and processes all pack files
  • Step 3/4: Updating packs - Merges JSON files and resolves conflicts
  • Step 4/4: Finalizing - Creates the final output packs
Wait for all steps to complete. The file list will clear automatically when done.
help.usage8_desc:
After merging, test the merged pack in Minecraft:
• Import both resource_pack.zip and behavior_pack.zip into Minecraft
• Activate them in your world settings
• Test all features to ensure everything works correctly
• If issues occur, remove problematic addons and re-merge
help.merge1_desc:
AutoBE creates two separate pack files:
• resource_pack.zip - Contains textures, sounds, models, and UI elements
• behavior_pack.zip - Contains entities, items, blocks, scripts, and game logic
Both files are required and must be imported into Minecraft together.
help.merge2_desc:
When addons have conflicting identifiers or files, AutoBE automatically resolves them:
• Conflicting identifiers are renamed to prevent collisions
• All references are updated automatically
• Your addons will work together without manual editing

Intelligent File Merging: AutoBE intelligently merges files when multiple addons modify the same files:
• Entity files: Combines components, component_groups, events, spawn_rules, and behaviors from all addons
• Player.json: Merges animations, render_controllers, and other properties intelligently
• JSON files: Recursively merges dictionaries and combines lists to preserve functionality from all addons

IMPORTANT: While AutoBE merges files intelligently, some conflicts may still occur if addons modify the same properties in incompatible ways. Always test merged packs thoroughly.
help.merge3_desc:
AutoBE can merge various addon file types:
• JSON configuration files (items, entities, blocks, recipes, etc.)
• Translation files (.lang)
• Script files (.mcfunction and JavaScript)
• Textures, models, and other assets
All files are properly merged and organized in the output packs.
help.merge4_desc:
If an addon contains multiple subpacks:
• You'll be prompted to select which subpack to include
• Choose the one you want to use in your merged pack
• Only one subpack per addon can be included
help.merge5_desc:
The merged pack automatically uses:
• The highest version requirements from all input packs
• All necessary dependencies and modules
• Proper format versions for compatibility
help.error1_desc:
CAUSE: The selected file is not a valid .mcpack file or is corrupted.
SOLUTION: 
• Ensure you're selecting .mcpack files, not .zip or other formats
• Try opening the file in Minecraft first to verify it's valid
• Re-download the addon if it's corrupted
• Check that the file isn't password-protected or encrypted
help.error2_title: Error: Version Mismatch / Addon Not Working
help.error2_desc:
CAUSE: Merging addons from different Minecraft versions causes compatibility issues.
SOLUTION: 
• Use 'Check Packs' to verify all addon versions
• ONLY merge addons from the same version (e.g., all 1.20 or all 1.21)
• Merging different versions is NOT RECOMMENDED and will likely cause problems
• Create separate merged packs for each version - never mix versions in one merge
help.error3_title: Error: Addon Features Not Working After Merge
help.error3_desc:
CAUSE: Identifier conflicts, file conflicts, or incompatible addons.
SOLUTION: 
• Test each addon individually first to identify the problematic one
• Check if multiple addons modify the same files (like player.json or entity files)
• When addons modify the same file, they may conflict - try merging them separately
• Remove the problematic addon and re-merge the rest
• Check Minecraft's error logs for specific error messages
help.error4_desc:
CAUSE: Resource pack files not properly merged or missing references.
SOLUTION: 
• Ensure you're importing BOTH resource_pack.zip AND behavior_pack.zip
• Check that all addons included their resource packs
• Some addons may be behavior-only - they won't have textures
• Verify the output directory contains both pack files
help.error5_desc:
CAUSE: JavaScript import conflicts or missing dependencies.
SOLUTION: 
• AutoBE handles most script conflicts automatically
• Ensure all addons are from the same Minecraft version
• Check that the manifest includes required script modules
• Some scripts may require specific API versions
help.error6_desc:
CAUSE: The addon contains multiple subpacks and you need to choose one.
SOLUTION: 
• A selection window will appear automatically
• Read the subpack names and descriptions
• Select the subpack you want to include
• If unsure, test each subpack individually first
help.error7_title: Error: Process Freezes or Takes Too Long
help.error7_desc:
CAUSE: Processing very large packs or too many packs at once.
SOLUTION: 
• The UI may appear frozen but processing continues in the background
• Wait for the progress bar to complete all 4 steps
• Try merging fewer packs at a time (5-10 packs per merge)
• Close other applications to free up system resources
help.error8_desc:
CAUSE: Output directory permissions or disk space issues.
SOLUTION: 
• Ensure the output directory is writable
• Check that you have enough disk space
• Try selecting a different output directory
• Run the application as administrator if permission errors occur
help.practice1: Always test addons individually before merging to identify any issues
help.practice2: Use 'Check Packs' to verify version compatibility before merging
help.practice3: Merge addons in small batches (5-10 packs) rather than all at once
help.practice4: Keep backups of original addon files before merging
help.practice5: Test merged packs immediately after creation to catch issues early
help.practice6: Organize addons by version in separate folders for easier management
help.practice7: If a merged pack has issues, remove problematic addons one by one to identify the culprit
help.practice8: Read addon descriptions/comments to understand compatibility requirements
help.practice9: Be aware that addons modifying the same files (player.json, entity files, spawning rules) may conflict
help.practice10: If multiple addons affect the same game mechanics (mob spawning, entity behavior), test carefully
help.practice11: Check if addons modify the same JSON files - these are more likely to have conflicts
help.practice12: Keep track of which addons you've merged together for easier troubleshooting
help.proc1_desc: AutoBE analyzes all your selected packs and creates a unified manifest file that combines all necessary information and version requirements.
help.proc2_desc: All pack files are extracted and organized. Resource packs and behavior packs are separated automatically.
help.proc3_desc: Files are merged together, conflicts are resolved automatically, and all references are updated to work correctly.
help.proc4_desc: The final pack files are created and prepared for use in Minecraft. Both resource_pack.zip and behavior_pack.zip are generated.
help.disc1_desc: This section contains important information to help you use AutoBE effectively and avoid common issues. For legal terms, license agreements, and policies, please refer to the Terms of Service & License Agreement that you accepted when first launching the application.
help.disc2_desc:
Always test addons individually before merging to ensure they work correctly. After merging, test the merged pack thoroughly to verify all features function as expected. Some addons may be incompatible with others due to game mechanic conflicts - AutoBE handles technical conflicts (identifier conflicts, file merging) but cannot resolve gameplay incompatibilities.

File Conflict Awareness: When multiple addons modify the same files (like player.json, entity files, or spawning rules), AutoBE merges them together. However, if addons modify the same properties in conflicting ways, some functionality may not work. If you experience issues like mobs not spawning, entities behaving strangely, or features not working, check if multiple addons in your merge affect the same game files or mechanics.
help.disc3_desc: Merging addons from different game versions is NOT RECOMMENDED. Each addon is designed to work with a specific version of Minecraft, and merging them from different versions can lead to conflicts, outdated code, missing dependencies, and compatibility issues. Always merge addons from the same version to ensure compatibility and avoid problems. Create separate merged packs for each version if you have addons from multiple versions.
help.disc4_desc: Always respect addon creators' terms of service and licensing agreements. Each creator has different rules regarding use, distribution, modification, and commercial use of their addons. It's your responsibility to review and comply with each addon creator's terms before using their addons with AutoBE.
help.disc5_desc: For technical support, questions, or assistance, contact us via email at thebedrocklabhelp@gmail.com or join our Discord community 'TheCodeNex'. You can also reach our owners directly: FrostyHostMC (Owner) or Eldas (Co-Owner) on Discord.
help.disc6_desc: For information about refunds, bans, prohibited activities, license terms, and other legal matters, please refer to the Terms of Service & License Agreement accessible from the initial startup window or by reviewing the legal documentation provided with your purchase.
